steps.title: Translate "at least twice" into an inequality
steps.content: The phrase "x is at least twice the value of y" breaks into two parts: "at least" means $\geq$, and "twice the value of y" means $2y$. Together: $x \geq 2y$.

steps.title: Substitute y = 47
steps.content: Replace y with 47: $x \geq 2(47) = 94$. The boundary value is 94.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: $x \geq 45$ — 45 is $47 - 2$, not twice 47. Eliminated.
  • B: $x \geq 49$ — 49 is $47 + 2$, not twice 47. Eliminated.
  • C: $x \geq 94$ — $2 \times 47 = 94$. This is exactly "at least twice y." That matches Choice C — $x \geq 94$.
  • D: $x \geq 96$ — 96 is $2(47) + 2$, off by 2. Eliminated.
